nodes.label: AI as practical problem-solving guide for hands-on tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable for tasks requiring local knowledge or trust
nodes.grounding_span: I've used AI to help me find the right toilet seat I need to buy for the toilet we have, asked it how to properly restick a tile to the wall

nodes.label: AI reliability and accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: AI knowledge and interpretations aren't always completely accurate

nodes.label: AI as upfront information provider with step-by-step instructions
nodes.label_negative: AI requiring iterative back-and-forth for basic information
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to get all of the information upfront presented in an organized list of logical steps

nodes.label: AI integration into clerical and inventory tasks
nodes.label_negative: Manual handling of clerical and inventory tasks
nodes.grounding_span: Perhaps we could someday integrate our inventory sheets and somehow have AI automatically order everything on the list from the correct suppliers

nodes.label: AI adoption in workplace culture
nodes.label_negative: Resistance to AI adoption by colleagues
nodes.grounding_span: My boyfriend handles pretty much everything else that I don't... but he's not as open-minded on the tech front

nodes.label: Practical competence and task completion
nodes.grounding_span: I have the physical ability to complete those simple-enough tasks even though I wasn't sure of the processes

nodes.label: Trust and reliability in information
nodes.grounding_span: AI knowledge and interpretations aren't always completely accurate

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for practical problem-solving
nodes.grounding_span: Most of the time, AI usage works out for me without giving me grief

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's obvious errors
nodes.grounding_span: I have been frustrated by AI's occasional failings... it kept referring to the colors in the photo I sent as shades of red when they were shades of dark and light greens

nodes.label: Nervousness about using AI for vendor selection
nodes.grounding_span: Using AI to try out new companies would make me nervous

nodes.label: Optimism about AI for inventory automation
nodes.grounding_span: Perhaps we could someday integrate our inventory sheets and somehow have AI automatically order everything

nodes.label: Preference for upfront structured information before execution
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to get all of the information upfront presented in an organized list of logical steps

nodes.label: Reliance on established relationships and word-of-mouth over AI recommendations
nodes.grounding_span: word-of-mouth between restauranteurs in this town is everything
